subject: Statistics
title: Economic Forecasting Failures
description: A 60-second lesson on Economic Forecasting Failures.

hook.text: In 2007, the Federal Reserve forecast continued economic growth. The worst recession since 1929 hit months later.

buildup.text: Economic forecasters have missed almost every recession. The track record for turning-point prediction is poor.

discovery.text: Economies are driven by human behavior, policy, and shocks. Models capture averages, not surprises.

twist.text: Forecasters are accurate during stable times — which is when you least need a forecast.

climax.text: The 2008 crisis showed that aggregate models missed systemic risk hiding in interconnected financial networks.

punchline.text: Forecasts work best when you don't need them. Surprises matter most.

quiz.question: Why do economic forecasters consistently fail to predict recessions?
options: Economies are driven by surprises and human behavior that models can't capture during stable periods
Recessions are caused by weather events
